schemas.name: Virtual Auditor

schemas.name: Trademark Opposition
schemas.description: Trademark opposition services. File opposition against published marks or defend your mark against opposition. Trade Marks Act Section 21. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Monitor Trademark Journal for conflicting marks
step.name: Step 1

step.text: File Notice of Opposition (TM-O) within 4 months
step.name: Step 2

mainEntity.name: What is the deadline to file trademark opposition?

acceptedAnswer.text: 4 months from the date of publication in the Trade Marks Journal.

mainEntity.name: Difference between trademark objection and opposition?

acceptedAnswer.text: Objection is raised by the Trademark Examiner during examination. Opposition is filed by a third party after publication. Different procedures and grounds.

mainEntity.name: How long does the opposition process take?

acceptedAnswer.text: 12-24 months depending on complexity and hearing schedule.

mainEntity.name: What is trademark opposition and who can file it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Opposition is a challenge filed by any person (including companies) against a published trademark application under Section 21 of the Trade Marks Act, 1999. Filed within 4 months of publication in the Trademark Journal.

mainEntity.name: What are the grounds for trademark opposition?

acceptedAnswer.text: Prior use/registration of identical/similar mark (Section 11). Bad faith filing. Descriptiveness (Section 9). Deceptive similarity. Well-known mark infringement. Prior copyright or design rights. Any ground on which registration could be refused.

mainEntity.name: What is the timeline for trademark opposition proceedings?

acceptedAnswer.text: Notice of opposition: within 4 months of publication. Counter-statement by applicant: 2 months from notice. Evidence filing: 2 months each for opponent and applicant. Hearing. Total: 12-24 months.

mainEntity.name: What is the cost of filing trademark opposition?

acceptedAnswer.text: Government fee: ₹2,500 per class. Professional fee: ₹10,000-₹25,000 depending on complexity. Evidence preparation and hearing costs additional. Full proceedings may cost ₹25,000-₹75,000 total.

The trademark application is refused. The applicant can appeal to the Intellectual Property Appellate Board (IPAB) within 3 months. If no appeal, the application is dead and the mark remains unregistered.
